Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

commit.message:
Fix match engine not evaluating new users

Root cause: Two filters excluded newly onboarded users from matching:

1. profile_state never set to 'live' — neither saveUserSurveyData nor
   the /api/survey/save route updated partnership profile_state when
   survey reached 100%. Partnerships stayed 'draft' forever.

2. display_name IS NOT NULL filter — match engine required non-null
   display_name, but onboarding never sets it. This excluded valid
   partnerships with completed surveys.

Fix:
- Set profile_state='live' on the partnership when survey hits 100%
  (in both save paths: server action and API route)
- Remove display_name IS NOT NULL filter from match engine candidate
  and recompute queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Owner Dashboard — Whop-like admin panel for site owner

New /owner.html page with 4 tabs:
- Overview: MRR, revenue, active members, churn rate, submissions stats
- Members: List all Whop members, search/filter, pause/cancel actions
- Submissions: Formspree-like form viewer with search, export CSV, delete
- Analytics: Live visitors, traffic summary, hourly chart, conversion funnel

Backend changes:
- Modified requireAdmin to accept owner member JWT (from 2FA login)
- Added requireOwnerMember middleware
- New endpoints: /api/owner/overview, /api/owner/members, /api/owner/revenue,
  /api/owner/members/:id/pause, /api/owner/members/:id/cancel
- Whop member list cached 2min, revenue cached 5min
- Revenue from Whop Payments API with fallback to MRR estimation

Owner dashboard link shown on member dashboard for bartwill452@gmail.com.

Fix SSE connection drops during long vignette generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix loadout sync: use dedicated sync-loadout event that bypasses slot unlock check

The equip-upgrade handler validates slot unlocks, which silently rejects
loadout items for slots not in the server's unlockedSlots array. The new
sync-loadout event sets the full loadout directly (with upgrade ID validation
only), fixing the desync that prevented welding_gun from being recognized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: simplify stockfish worker — importScripts is sufficient

stockfish.js@10 is a self-contained UCI worker; it wires its own
onmessage/postMessage handlers during importScripts. The STOCKFISH()
factory call was a misread of the API, caused a ReferenceError (since
STOCKFISH is not a global in this version), and accidentally worked
because stockfish's handlers were already live before the crash.

Removing lines 2-4 eliminates the console error with no behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix region capture on Wayland showing portal dialog instead of crosshair

CaptureRegionAsync had an early return for Wayland that bypassed the
in-app crosshair overlay entirely, delegating to the platform's
CaptureRegionAsync which calls the XDG Screenshot portal with
interactive=true. The portal only offers full-screen or window
selection, not a free-form region draw.

Remove both the early-return bypass and the null-background fallback.
The normal flow now:
1. Attempts a silent full-screen background capture (non-interactive
   portal, same path the ruler/tools already use successfully).
2. Shows the XerahS crosshair overlay with the frozen background.
3. If the background was unavailable, shows the crosshair with a
   dark/transparent bg and captures the cropped region afterwards via
   a non-interactive CaptureRectAsync call.

This aligns region capture behaviour with SelectRegionAsync (used by
the ruler and recording tools) which already works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(tools,guard): attachment processing, thread ID validation, and guard environment tests

Messaging:
- Added is_valid_explicit_thread_id() that rejects bare numeric thread
  IDs, which are reserved for reply-seeded threads. This prevents
  accidental collision between explicit and auto-generated thread IDs.
- Added process_message_attachments() for handling file attachments in
  messages: processes markdown image references, copies attachment files
  to the archive directory, and generates attachment metadata. Supports
  configurable embed policies and markdown image conversion.

Reservations:
- Extended reservation handling with additional validation and improved
  conflict detection logic.

Tests:
- contact_policy_parity: Expanded parity tests to cover new contact
  policy scenarios and edge cases.
- validation_error_parity: Added new test cases for validation error
  consistency across the tools layer.

Guard:
- Enhanced guard module with additional environment-aware checks and
  configurable enforcement levels.
- Added guard_env_tests: New test suite for environment-dependent guard
  behavior, verifying correct enforcement across different runtime
  configurations.

commit.message:
Apple 로그인 signInWithPopup으로 전환 및 에러 로깅 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix per-pane command guard pack override, case-insensitive snippet extraction, and mouse scroll panic

command_guard.rs:
- Pass PaneGuardConfig to keyword_filter() so per-pane enabled_packs
  overrides are respected during the keyword pre-filter phase. Previously
  keyword_filter() always used the global policy's enabled_packs, meaning
  a pane configured with only "core.git" would still match keywords from
  "core.filesystem" and other globally-enabled packs, causing incorrect
  blocking decisions. Now when a pane has an explicit enabled_packs list,
  only those packs participate in keyword matching.
- Add per_pane_enabled_packs_override test verifying that a pane with
  only core.git enabled allows rm -rf (filesystem pack not active for
  that pane) while still blocking git push --force (git pack active).

tantivy_query.rs:
- Replace manual to_lowercase() substring search with
  regex::RegexBuilder case_insensitive(true) for snippet term matching.
  The previous approach used text.to_lowercase().find(term_lower) which
  could produce incorrect byte offsets when the original text contains
  multi-byte characters whose lowercase form has different byte length
  (e.g., Turkish İ → i̇ changes byte count). The regex approach matches
  against the original text directly, producing correct byte positions.
- Use the regex match length (m.end() - m.start()) instead of term.len()
  for highlight marker placement, since case-insensitive matching may
  find a span with different byte length than the search term.

mouseevent.rs:
- Replace two unwrap() calls on self.terminal_size.rows.try_into() with
  unwrap_or(isize::MAX), preventing a panic if the terminal row count
  cannot be converted to isize. This is defensive — the value is
  typically small — but the unwrap was in a scroll event handler where a
  panic would crash the GUI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): guard memLimitPrefix against missing systemd user session

memLimitPrefix() previously checked only runtime.GOOS == "linux" before
emitting a systemd-run --user prefix. In Docker containers, devcontainers,
WSL1, and other environments without a systemd user session, this causes
agent launch to fail because systemd-run --user cannot connect to the
user session bus.

Add a sync.OnceValue guard that probes for the per-user systemd private
socket (/run/user/<uid>/systemd/private) before enabling the prefix.
When the socket is absent, memLimitPrefix() returns an empty string,
allowing agents to launch without cgroup memory limiting.

Fixes #85

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix string structRef leaking into SQL interpolation segments (#2697)

* Hash raw config file content instead of building JSON object

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix string structRef leaking into SQL interpolation segments

When a source uses %{ source -> view } SQL interpolation, the query
inside stores its source reference as a bare string name. After import
into another model, that name can't be resolved, causing "Unexpected
reference to an undefined source". Fix by passing isRefOk=false in
sql-string.ts to force inline StructDef instead of string names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add alias to SQL interpolation subquery for MySQL compatibility

MySQL requires derived tables to have aliases. The test SQL was missing
an AS alias after the %{ } interpolation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix test SQL for cross-dialect compatibility and remove unused devDependency

Quote identifiers in sql interpolation test for Snowflake case-folding,
add derived table alias for MySQL. Remove unused db-databricks devDep.

---------

commit.message:
feat(share): verify-live pipeline with fail-fast, probe refactoring, and deploy robustness

Deploy module:
- Added fail-fast mode to run_verify_live(): when enabled, aborts after
  local stage if any Error-severity check fails, skipping remote and
  security stages for faster feedback loops.
- Extracted helper functions (is_root_derived_remote_check,
  build_probe_check_result, run_single_probe_check) to reduce
  cyclomatic complexity in the main verification pipeline.
- Added SqliteConnection import for persistent verification state
  tracking across deploy sessions.

Probe module:
- Major refactoring of probe check evaluation with clearer separation
  between check definition, execution, and result evaluation.
- evaluate_probe_check() and probe_error_result() extracted as public
  functions to support reuse from deploy module.
- Improved error categorization and timeout handling in probe requests.

Bundle module:
- Enhanced bundle creation with additional validation steps, improved
  manifest generation, and better error messages for malformed assets.

Finalize module:
- Refined post-deploy finalization with additional cleanup steps and
  verification of deployed state.

Planner: Minor adjustment to planning logic.
Lib: Added new public exports for probe evaluation functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove 'c' from ALL_LANGS to prevent doctor 404 on nonexistent ubs-c.sh module

The ALL_LANGS array included 'c' as a separate entry, but there is no
ubs-c.sh module — C files are handled by the cpp module (ubs-cpp.sh).
The normalize_lang() function already maps 'c' → 'cpp' at scan time,
but the doctor command's module download loop doesn't use
normalize_lang(), causing a 404 when trying to download ubs-c.sh.

Fixes #34

feat(server): health signature headers, PID-aware port clearing, and UI improvements

Startup checks:
- Added HEALTH_SIGNATURE_HEADER_NAME/VALUE constants for identifying
  Agent Mail health check responses vs other HTTP servers on the same
  port. The health check probe now validates this header to
  definitively distinguish Agent Mail from other services.
- Refactored is_agent_mail_health_check() into a closure-based
  pattern for cleaner early-return control flow.
- Port clearing now supports PID-hint based process identification,
  using a dedicated pid-hint directory to map ports to process IDs.
  This enables targeted SIGTERM instead of blind port-based killing.
- Added IpAddr import for more precise bind address handling.

Integrity guard:
- Enhanced background integrity check scheduling with configurable
  intervals and full vs quick check distinction. Improved error
  reporting and metric integration.

Mail UI:

  calculations, and more informative health/metrics panels.

commit.message:
Add fcp-google-discovery crate, baseline study documents, and workspace integration

New crate: fcp-google-discovery
- auth.rs: Google OAuth2 integration for Discovery API access
- generator.rs: Code generation from Google Discovery document schemas,
  producing typed Rust connector surfaces from service descriptions
- executor.rs: Runtime execution layer for generated Google API operations
- policy.rs: Policy engine mapping Discovery methods to capability/risk/
  safety/approval rules per the security baseline
- data/google_policy_matrix.v1.json: Helper overlay policy matrix defining
  strict allow criteria for handwritten helpers above generated operations,
  forbidden patterns, and the initial high-value workflow shortlist
- data/fixtures/: Gmail Discovery v1 fixture and unknown-service test fixture
  for deterministic testing without network access
- data/golden/: BLAKE3 hash golden file for Gmail generated artifact
  stability verification

Study documents (normative baselines):
- STUDY_GOOGLE_Discovery_Service_Resolution.md: Defines the tiny curated
  alias registry, explicit service:version fallback behavior, deterministic
  snapshot/cache naming identity, and strict separation between service
  resolution and method catalog generation
- STUDY_GOOGLE_Security_Policy_Mapping.md: Maps Discovery methods to
  capability/risk/safety/approval rules, recommended zone envelopes and
  host allowlist defaults, service-specific carve-outs, and fail-closed
  treatment for unmapped methods
- STUDY_GOOGLE_Gmail_Calendar_Baseline_Audit.md: Documents current
  duplication points, service-owned semantics that should remain
  handwritten, and case-by-case decisions for substrate migration

Requirements index updates:
- Link all three study documents and the helper overlay policy to their
  owner beads in the Google connector foundation section
- Cross-reference security/policy mapping, helper overlay, and baseline
  audit baselines for downstream migration bead work

Workspace changes:
- Add fcp-google-discovery to Cargo.toml members, default-members, and
  workspace dependency table
- Add tokio rt dependency to fcp-async-core for runtime compatibility
- Update Cargo.lock with fcp-google-discovery and its dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(storage): robust coalescer commit pipeline with structured outcomes and failure tracking

Rewrites the git coalescer commit pipeline to use structured outcome
types instead of fire-and-forget semantics:

- Added CoalescerCommitOutcome and CoalescerSpillOutcome structs that
  track committed_requests, committed_commits, and failed_requests
  separately, replacing the implicit "assume success" pattern.

- coalescer_commit_batch() now returns CoalescerCommitOutcome, allowing
  the pool worker to handle failures explicitly — failed requests
  increment error counters and their latency is tracked separately
  from successful commits.

- CoalescerCommitFields derives Clone to support the failure retry path
  where failed requests may be re-queued or logged.

- Metrics recording moved inside success/failure branches: drained_total
  and commits_total only increment on actual success, and error_total
  increments per-failed-request rather than per-batch.

- Commit latency tracking now properly distinguishes successful vs
  failed request latencies, preventing failed requests from polluting
  p50/p99 latency percentiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: WebSocket reconnect with exponential backoff for rc-agent

Agent no longer dies on first disconnect. Wraps WebSocket connection
and main event loop in a reconnection loop with 1s→30s exponential
backoff. All local state (lock screen, kiosk, game process, HID/UDP
monitors) persists across reconnections. Shows "CONNECTION LOST"
screen during reconnection attempts (skipped if customer is mid-session).

Also refactors non-AC sim handling to avoid early return, making all
sim types benefit from the reconnection logic.
